# preciousmetalscharts

> Independent live price charts for precious metals (gold, silver, platinum, palladium),
> with the gold-to-silver ratio, a dealer-premium calculator, a private portfolio tracker
> and free price alerts. Independent and not affiliated with any dealer or mint.
> Educational information only — not investment advice.

## Key facts
- Metals covered: gold (XAU), silver (XAG), platinum (XPT), palladium (XPD).
- Data is referenced to global spot markets (COMEX futures and the London/LBMA market).
- Spot price = the reference price for one troy ounce; physical coins and bars cost more
  because dealers add a premium over spot.
- Premium over spot = your price minus the spot value of the metal, shown in money and as a
  percentage. Premiums are larger on small coins than large bars, and larger on silver than gold.
- Gold-to-silver ratio = gold price ÷ silver price; a high ratio is read as silver being
  cheap relative to gold. Historical range: under ~35 at silver peaks to above ~100 in
  market stress.
- The site is independent: it sells no metals and holds no inventory.
- Historical prices go back to the 1970s (palladium to 1998), built from the site's own price
  archive with deep history from open World Bank commodity data (Pink Sheet, CC BY 4.0). Each
  metal page exposes its series as a schema.org Dataset.

## Pages
- [Charts (home)](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/): live spot prices, main chart, ratio,
  premiums, performance comparison, FAQ.
- Live metal price pages — live price, exact per-ounce/gram/kilo conversions, a 50-year history
  chart and a "what moves the price" explainer for each metal:
  [Gold](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/gold-price),
  [Silver](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/silver-price),
  [Platinum](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/platinum-price),
  [Palladium](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/palladium-price). Prices show in USD or EUR.
- [Calculators](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/calculators): a hub of free gold/silver tools,
  several powered by the 50-year price archive (rare among bullion sites):
  - [Premium over spot](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/premium-calculator): metal + weight +
    price paid (USD/EUR) → premium in money and percent vs the live spot price.
  - [DCA backtest](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/dca-calculator): what investing a fixed
    amount every month into a metal since the 1970s would be worth today (buys at spot;
    excludes premiums/fees/taxes; historical illustration, not a prediction).
  - [Gold vs cash purchasing power](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/purchasing-power-calculator):
    how much gold-buying power cash has lost since a chosen year, and what holding gold would have done.
  - [Gold-to-silver ratio strategy](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/ratio-strategy-calculator):
    backtests switching between gold and silver at ratio extremes, with a cost-per-switch setting
    that shows how dealer spreads erode the apparent edge. Educational, not advice.
- [Market recap](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/market-recap): an auto-generated, factual weekly recap of how the metals moved, the gold-to-silver ratio and where prices stand. Updated daily.
- Price history by year — yearly average, high, low, % change and a chart for each year, built
  from our own archive (deep history: World Bank Pink Sheet, CC BY 4.0):
  [Gold price history](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/gold-price-history) and
  [Silver price history](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/silver-price-history), with a page per
  year (e.g. https://preciousmetalscharts.com/gold-price-2008).
- [Gold-to-silver ratio](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/ratio): the live ratio plus its
  full history back to the 1970s, with a plain-language guide to what it means.
- [Where to buy gold & silver](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/buy): independent guide to
  buying — physical coins and bars, vaulted (allocated) metal and savings plans — with
  premiums, storage and how to compare providers.
- Trust & policy: [About](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/about),
  [Methodology](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/methodology),
  [Disclaimer](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/disclaimer),
  [Affiliate disclosure](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/affiliate-disclosure).

## Definitions worth quoting
- Spot price: the current reference price for immediate settlement of one troy ounce of
  unfabricated metal, set continuously by global trading (COMEX, London/LBMA).
- Premium: the markup over spot a dealer charges for a physical coin or bar, covering minting,
  fabrication, distribution and margin.
- Troy ounce: the standard unit for precious metals; one troy ounce = 31.1035 grams.

## Contact / authority
- Publisher: preciousmetalscharts
- Editorial policy: scores follow fixed weights set before any commission terms are considered.
- Disclosure: some outbound links are affiliate links; they never influence scoring.
